articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Surge in Concerns**: 82% of landlords are worried about the Renters' Rights Act, with nearly half (44%) expressing significant concerns.  
- **Market Shift**: 75% of landlords feel regulations deter portfolio growth, and almost 50% are considering selling or downsizing within two months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords face pressure to enhance EPC ratings to appeal to cost-conscious tenants amid rising energy prices stemming from the Iran War.  
- Upcoming regulations will require rental properties to meet a minimum C rating by 2030, prompting landlords to prioritize energy efficiency improvements.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Less than 25% of UK landlords believe that planned reforms will benefit their operations.  
- Lack of confidence in planning changes raises concerns for the future of the rental market.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- Over 58% of hidden leaks in UK properties start from external sources, according to leak detection expert Aspect.  
- Landlords are urged to prioritize maintenance of outside pipework to avoid costly damages.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters’ Rights Act, effective May 1, 2026, will introduce significant changes in the Private Rented Sector, emphasizing a more equitable balance between landlord and tenant rights.  
- Landlords should prepare for the upcoming regulatory shifts by following key tips to ensure compliance with the new requirements.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- A new survey reveals that 84% of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act launching on May 1.  
- Alarmingly, 58% of them are at high risk of non-compliance with the upcoming regulations.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are shifting strategies from expansion to restructuring portfolios and financing in light of slowing rental growth, as indicated by the ONS announcement.  
- The focus is now on optimizing existing assets to align with changing rental demand rather than acquiring new properties.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey reveals landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the housing market.  
- The increasing dominance of investors is exacerbating affordability issues for new entrants to the property market.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the incoming government to acknowledge the private rented sector's significance with a clear strategy.  
- A formal recognition is essential to curb the declining landlord presence in Scotland.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- New HMRC regulations require mandatory registration for tax advisors, impacting conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams starting May.
- Non-compliance can result in hefty penalties of up to £10,000 for landlords and affected professionals.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council argues that implementing licensing for landlords won't result in increased rent prices.
- Landlords are projected to incur a cost of £694 per property, with the council not expecting to recoup this expense.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook confirms the party will not implement rent controls if elected, emphasizing a different approach to housing issues.  
- The clarification comes as part of Labour's broader commitment to addressing the UK's housing crisis without imposing rent restrictions.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council aims to regulate the burgeoning "HMO landlord free for all" phenomenon to enhance oversight.  
- Detailed guidelines for Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) are being developed to ensure better management and compliance.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long campaign starts Monday to educate tenants on leveraging their rights under the Renters Rights Act.
- The initiative aims to empower renters with knowledge and tools to navigate their rental agreements effectively.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters Rights Act is leading to a surge in Section 21 evictions, as anticipated by expert Paul Shamplina.  
- Landlords are increasingly taking action to evict tenants amidst the changing rental landscape.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are on the rise as landlords adjust to upcoming changes from the Renters Rights Act, according to new data from Hamptons.
- The report highlights a trend in preemptive rent increases as the housing market braces for regulatory shifts aimed at enhancing tenant protections.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act, aimed at enhancing tenant protections, is expected to significantly deter hobbyist landlords from entering the rental market.  
- Critics argue that the new regulations will create an unwelcoming environment for casual landlords, potentially leading to a housing shortage.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly diving into property investment, showcasing a growing interest in the real estate market despite challenges.  
- The trend persists even with heightened regulations and tax burdens impacting property investors in the UK.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Sellers are facing pressure to reduce asking prices, cancel sales, or leave the market entirely, according to the House Buyer Bureau.
- Market conditions are tough, forcing sellers to navigate a challenging landscape, highlights Chris Hodgkinson.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is increasing, boosting Inheritance Tax receipts.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC intensifies scrutiny of property valuations, leading to a spike in referrals to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).  
- This increased oversight is contributing to a rise in Inheritance Tax receipts.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.
The post Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A terrace property in Penrith, Cumbria, is attracting attention after listing photos and videos mistakenly captured a sleeping tenant in bed.  
- The £135,000 property’s unusual marketing moment has sparked conversations about tenant privacy and real estate practices.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Conveyancing Task Force reveals referral fees act as an "unseen surcharge," adding to the financial burden on homebuyers.  
- These fees are criticized as a "hidden tax," raising concerns about transparency in property transactions.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.
The post Lomond expands operation north of the border appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lomond is expanding its operations into Scotland, with Clyde Property Group taking charge of its West of Scotland portfolio.  
- David Alexander announces strategic growth, positioning Lomond to strengthen its presence in the region.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons reports a dramatic 35% drop in sales revenue due to unexpected low buyer activity amid geopolitical challenges.  
- CEO Guy Gittins cites external factors as the primary cause for the downturn in the property market.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Inflation in the UK is increasing, with significant price surges in fuel and transport due to the ongoing Middle East conflict.  
- Experts warn that these rising costs are likely to soon affect broader sectors of the economy.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Majority of New Builds Go Unlisted:** A staggering nine out of ten newly constructed homes never make it to the open sales market, highlighting a significant gap in real estate visibility.  
- **Industry Insight from Alto CEO:** Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson emphasizes the need for awareness regarding this trend in housing availability.
